question: Across years 2015-16 through 2018-19, which Texas counties are consistently in the top five for total summer SSO lunch serves? Among those counties, which two saw the largest percentage decreases in day care home meal reimbursements from 2018-19 to 2020-21? Of those two, which had the higher average child center enrollment across 2018-19 and 2019-20? Return the county name only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: In the 2015-2016 SSO meal count program period dataset, the top five counties by LunchTotal were Harris (675,945), Hidalgo (651,134), Dallas (341,323), Cameron (325,806), and El Paso (262,063).
1.subquestion: Which Texas counties were in the top five for SSO LunchTotal in every program year from 2015-2016 through 2018-2019? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2015-2016; group by SiteCounty; sum LunchTotal; rank by total; keep top 5.)

2.fact: In the 2016-2017 SSO meal count program period dataset, the top five counties by LunchTotal were Hidalgo (716,795), Harris (614,713), Dallas (376,028), Cameron (309,590), and Bexar (278,760).
2.subquestion: Which Texas counties were in the top five for SSO LunchTotal in every program year from 2015-2016 through 2018-2019? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2016-2017; group by SiteCounty; sum LunchTotal; rank by total; keep top 5.)

3.fact: In the 2017-2018 SSO meal count program period dataset, the top five counties by LunchTotal were Hidalgo (667,415), Harris (480,251), Dallas (362,790), Cameron (258,875), and Bexar (235,800).
3.subquestion: Which Texas counties were in the top five for SSO LunchTotal in every program year from 2015-2016 through 2018-2019? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2017-2018; group by SiteCounty; sum LunchTotal; rank by total; keep top 5.)

4.fact: In the 2018-2019 SSO meal count program period dataset, the top five counties by LunchTotal were Hidalgo (668,927), Harris (525,238), Dallas (348,923), Bexar (284,028), and Cameron (260,791).
4.subquestion: Which Texas counties were in the top five for SSO LunchTotal in every program year from 2015-2016 through 2018-2019? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2018-2019; group by SiteCounty; sum LunchTotal; rank by total; keep top 5.)

5.fact: In the CACFP day care homes meal reimbursement dataset for ProgramYear 2018-2019, total reimbursements for Harris, Dallas, Hidalgo, and Cameron counties were: Harris $8,757,796.13; Dallas $2,835,278.75; Hidalgo $1,202,699.73; Cameron $392,489.51.
5.subquestion: Among {Harris, Hidalgo, Dallas, Cameron} (from hop 1), which counties had the largest percentage decrease in CACFP day care home meal reimbursements between the 2018-2019 and 2020-2021 program years? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2018-2019; group by SiteCounty; sum TotalMealReimbursement.)

6.fact: In the CACFP day care homes meal reimbursement dataset for ProgramYear 2020-2021, total reimbursements for Harris, Dallas, Hidalgo, and Cameron counties were: Harris $7,558,597.77; Dallas $2,393,411.26; Hidalgo $971,063.56; Cameron $330,003.06.
6.subquestion: Among {Harris, Hidalgo, Dallas, Cameron} (from hop 1), which counties had the largest percentage decrease in CACFP day care home meal reimbursements between the 2018-2019 and 2020-2021 program years? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2020-2021; group by SiteCounty; sum TotalMealReimbursement.)

7.fact: In the CACFP child centers dataset for ProgramYear 2018-2019, total EnrollmentQty was Cameron 7,620,183 and Hidalgo 1,661,128.
7.subquestion: Among {Hidalgo, Cameron} (from hop 2), which county had the higher average CACFP child-center EnrollmentQty across the 2018-2019 and 2019-2020 program years? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2018-2019; group by SiteCounty; sum EnrollmentQty.)

8.fact: In the CACFP child centers dataset for ProgramYear 2019-2020, total EnrollmentQty was Cameron 1,076,746 and Hidalgo 1,477,805.
8.subquestion: Among {Hidalgo, Cameron} (from hop 2), which county had the higher average CACFP child-center EnrollmentQty across the 2018-2019 and 2019-2020 program years? (Filter for this node: ProgramYear == 2019-2020; group by SiteCounty; sum EnrollmentQty.)

reasoning_chain: Node 1 (2015-2016): top five LunchTotal counties -> {Harris, Hidalgo, Dallas, Cameron, El Paso}
Node 2 (2016-2017): top five LunchTotal counties -> {Hidalgo, Harris, Dallas, Cameron, Bexar}
Node 3 (2017-2018): top five LunchTotal counties -> {Hidalgo, Harris, Dallas, Cameron, Bexar}
Node 4 (2018-2019): top five LunchTotal counties -> {Hidalgo, Harris, Dallas, Bexar, Cameron}
HOP 2 (d=2, CACFP day care homes reimbursements 2018-2019 vs 2020-2021 - nodes 5-6):
Percent decreases: Hidalgo -19.26%, Cameron -15.92%, Dallas -15.58%, Harris -13.69% -> two largest decreases {Hidalgo, Cameron}
HOP 3 (d=2, CACFP child centers EnrollmentQty 2018-2019 vs 2019-2020 - nodes 7-8):
Average EnrollmentQty: Cameron 4,348,464.5 vs Hidalgo 1,569,466.5 -> Cameron County
